heading: Additional prohibition on cruel, inhuman, or degrading treatment or punishment
label: 2000dd–0 Additional prohibition on cruel, inhuman, or degrading treatment or punishment
notes: CodificationSection was enacted as part of the Military Commissions Act of 2006, and not as part of the Detainee Treatment Act of 2005 which comprises this chapter.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: No individual in the custody or under the physical control of the United States Government, regardless of nationality or physical location, shall be subject to cruel, inhuman, or degrading treatment or punishment.

children.heading: Cruel, inhuman, or degrading treatment or punishment defined
children.label: 2 Cruel, inhuman, or degrading treatment or punishment defined
children.text: In this section, the term “cruel, inhuman, or degrading treatment or punishment” means cruel, unusual, and inhumane treatment or punishment prohibited by the Fifth, Eighth, and Fourteenth Amendments to the Constitution of the United States, as defined in the United States Reservations, Declarations and Understandings to the United Nations Convention Against Torture and Other Forms of Cruel, Inhuman or Degrading Treatment or Punishment done at New York, .December 10, 1984

children.heading: Compliance
children.label: 3 Compliance
children.text: The President shall take action to ensure compliance with this section, including through the establishment of administrative rules and procedures.
